title: Connection directly to IP address on internal network no longer working/removed from latest release.
body:
<!-- Hey there, thank you for creating an issue! -->

In the past I was able to enter an IP address into the connection number input and connect directly without a internet relay..
When I attemtp this now, it simply open window and tries to connect many times, timinig out and looping for about 3-5 times.

An option in the configuration menu use to have the option to enable direct IP connections.  This apears to have disappeared.


...
Expectations:
To be able to enter an IP address and directly connect to a rustdesk instance without the need for a relay host.

...


I have tried this with Linux, windows Mac.  using the current 1.1.8 version on each.

This looks more like the feature has been intentionally removed in 1.1.8
Can we please get an explanation as to why this has occured?
It appears counter intuitive to the goals of this tool.....

title: Feature Request - simplified configuration options
body:
I thought it would be nice if users could easily configure their Rustdesk client installations. 

To accomplish this, I would suggest registering the Rustdesk client as a log. For example: rustdesk://<_hostsystem_>/?key=<_pub_key_>.

This would allow the platform to configure the Rustdesk client independently without, for example, manipulating the client exe. 

On Windows, you can achieve this by adding a registry entry to register the application for this protocol.

title: Some complaints from `systemd` and `namcap`
body:
<!-- Hey there, thank you for creating an issue! -->

**Describe the bug you encountered:**
First, `systemd` complains about the unit service.
```
> systemctl status rustdesk.service
/usr/lib/systemd/system/rustdesk.service:9: PIDFile= references a path below legacy directory /var/run/, updating /var/run/rustdesk.pid → /run/rustdesk.pid; please update the unit file accordingly.
```
This line should be changed
https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/85a2a7cd4452502d16c10edf1ba81573f215ec7c/rustdesk.service#L9
to
```
PIDFile=/run/rustdesk.pid
```

Besides, `namcap` complains about the dependencies (**Only on arch and arch derived distributions**)
Please reconsider the dependencies.
```
rustdesk W: Dependency libxcb included but already satisfied
rustdesk W: Dependency libxfixes included but already satisfied
rustdesk W: Dependency included and not needed ('alsa-lib')
rustdesk W: Dependency included and not needed ('pulseaudio')
rustdesk W: Dependency included and not needed ('ttf-arphic-uming')
rustdesk W: Dependency included and not needed ('python-pip')
rustdesk W: Dependency included and not needed ('curl')
```

BTW, the file `/usr/share/rustdesk/files/pynput_service.py` should be put into `/usr/share/rustdesk/pynput_service.py`,
and `python-pynput` should be added as a dependency, instead of installing it via `sudo pip` in `postinstall`
...

**What did you expect to happen instead?**

...


**How did you install `RustDesk`?**
via aur package [rustdesk-bin](https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/rustdesk-bin), but these issues also occur in the officially released package.
<!-- GitHub release, build from source, Windows portable version, etc. -->


---

**RustDesk version and environment**

title: Chang the name of Windows executable
body:
The name of Windows executable contains "putes" (reverse of "setup"), which is unfortunately a vulgar term in several languages(French, Austrian etc). See https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/putes.  

Could it be changed to something else?

I guess the reason for avoiding the filename is due to this, https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11573444/why-is-windows-asking-for-system-administrator-privileges-for-running-executable. One answer provides a solution to use "setup" in the filename. 

Sorry I don't have a widows machine around, so I couldn't test the solution myself.

author: boris1971

title: uri and QR codes for quick connect to and also config self-hosted servers?
body:
Hi.

I just had a nice idea for a feature request -- we could implement handling of links with a scheme like such (see below):

  * rustdesk://123456789 -- ordinary connection link, public relay servers.
  * rustdesk://123456789?server=rd.example.org&key=FooBarBaz -- connection through a self-hosted server
  * rustdesk://?server=rd.example.org&key=FooBarBaz -- set self-hosted server parameters

The same thing could be encoded into a QR code which could be read by clients both from screen, camera, or file. I feel like this can be super useful in a lot of scenarios. For example:
  * Set up a client for a custom server quickly: just snap a QR with your smartphone or laptop camera and there you are, custom relay server is set automatically.
  * Help a novice user connect via a custom server without encoding it in client file name -- "Here, click this link, or click that button and let RustDesk grab the code from the screen for you"

Just like Wi-Fi QR codes, but for RustDesk.

Waiting for your comments and suggestions :)

title: URL handler
body:
Hi, In Windows there is a way to add URL handlers to allow opening an other program using an URL (mailto:\ or anydesk:\). I have managed to do it for rustdesk (until it's integrated with it) but needed to create a small independant URL handler to do that. The registry keys necessary for this are : 

Windows Registry Editor Version 5.00

[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Classes\RustDesk]
@="URL:RustDesk Protocol"
"URL Protocol"=""

[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Classes\RustDesk\DefaultIcon]
@="RustDesk.exe,0"

[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Classes\RustDesk\shell]

[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Classes\RustDesk\shell\open]

[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Classes\RustDesk\shell\open\command]
@="\"C:\\Program Files\\RustDesk\\urlhandler.exe\" \"%1\""
The small URL handler in powershell is 

$url_handler = $args[0
$rustdesk_id = $url_handler -creplace '(?s)^.*\:',''
Start-Process -NoNewWindow -FilePath "${Env:ProgramFiles}\RustDesk\rustdesk.exe" -ArgumentList "--connect $rustdesk_id"
I then convert the Powershell script to an exe file using ps2exe and put it on the same directory as rustdesk.exe 

Install-Module ps2exe
Invoke-ps2exe .\urlhandler.ps1 .\urlhandler.exe
When all this is done you can finally connect to a machine using the URL rustdesk:\ on a browser. Would be great to have an intergrated URL handler into rustdesk.exe that convert the information received from the browser (rustdesk:\) to just the \ (perhaps using a different command that connect (but that would do the same at the end) like -urlhandler %1 (which is rustdesk:\)

I've tried adding an allow rule in windows advanced firewall.

While connecting if flashes an error for about 0.5s that says: "deadline has elapsed".

title: --import-config
body:
**Describe the bug you encountered:**
how does --import-config work exactly? i have a pre-configured RustDesk2.toml:
```
rendezvous_server = ''
nat_type = 1
serial = 0

[options]
direct-server = 'Y'
stop-service = 'Y'
```

so i put a default RustDesk.toml and my pre-configured RustDesk2.toml in the location of the *.exe file
i try after install:
` .\rustdesk-1.1.9-putes.exe --import-config RustDesk.toml`

in the log i see:
`[2022-06-09 13:34:53.732761 +02:00] INFO [src\main.rs:152] import config from "RustDesk.toml" and "RustDesk2.toml"`


**What did you expect to happen instead?**
so i expect it loads the config but the setting is still not being applied, the GUI looks the same as before and the RustDesk2.toml in AppData\Roaming\RustDesk\config shows:
```
rendezvous_server = 'rs-ny.rustdesk.com'
nat_type = 1
serial = 0

[options]
```
so it seems nothing was imported.
...


**How did you install `RustDesk`?**

It's the official 1.1.9 release with installer on Win11

title: ID/Relay server popup closes immediately
body:
<!-- Hey there, thank you for creating an issue! -->

**Describe the bug you encountered:**
Once installed on Ubuntu 22.04, after the wayland deactivation procedure and a reboot, I tried to add my own relay server by selecting the id/relay server in the menu. Then a popup open but once I click/type anything, the popup closes immediately.
...

**What did you expect to happen instead?**
I expect to be able to define my own relay server using the menu.
...

title: Feature Request - DNS Service Record Lookup
body:
Allow the relay server input box to use the [DNS Service record](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SRV_record) for a host and port to connect to.

This would allow a "zero-touch" config feel for configuring the client and enable the clients to know what host and port to use to connect. Basically doing the same thing as Minecraft, Kerberos, SIP, and LDAP all support the use of service records via DNS.

title: How to forcefully change RustDesk client ID
body:
Hi, RustDesk Team!

Take for example we have a situation of 20 exactly the same personal computers in a work/school room. We install OS Windows 10, drivers, programs, full install of RustDesk 1.1.9, make lots of changes and configurations and then apply the same image to the rest of the PCs (to save time and resources) ... How exactly to change the ID of every of the cloned PCs (before or after that), because after imaging every one of them is having the same ID and pass ... In Teamviewer after cloning one PC to another it somehow finds out the hardware have changed (even a little bit - new CPU, SSD, Mainboard serial numbers) and therefore changes ID automatically, too ... In Anydesk if you delete C:\programdata\Anydesk folder before cloning and then after applying the image to the rest of the PCs and when starting each one we have different IDs, if you don't delete C:\programdata\Anydesk - ID is the same to all of the PCs ... I have experimented to delete C:\Users\User\AppData\Roaming\RustDesk folder before or after cloning (stopping RustDesk service first) with no success - always the same ID and pass ...

What to do in these cases?!

title: Wake up after sleep can't connect
body:
**Describe the bug you encountered:**

When you wake up after sleeping, there is a certain chance that you will not be able to connect, indicating that the ID is not online. After comparison:
There will be two UDP connections when online normally (because I installed the server on the router).
![2022-06-26_13-07-20](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/50667472/175800269-72af63b1-070a-4e84-85b3-ecab8bfebccc.png)

The UDP connection sent by the router is missing when the problem occurs.
![2022-06-26_12-37-23](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/50667472/175800230-bdb16d6e-06a9-4dd6-a17a-cf4c36c8d32f.png)

当睡眠后唤醒，有一定几率会连接不上，提示ID不在线，经过对比：
正常在线时会有两条UDP连接（因为我把服务器安装在路由器上）。
出现问题时缺少路由器发送过来的UDP连接。

**What did you expect to happen instead?**

Maybe add a check to make sure all connections are available.
My current approach is to schedule the task, restart the service and tray when a wake is detected.

title: Client configuration for self hosted server
body:
For Windows you are able to configure the host and the public key via the filename, which looks very suspicious.

`rustdesk-host=MY.DOMAIN,key=MY-PUBLIC-KEY=.exe`

It would be much more comfortable for a user to be able to use a configuration instead of this cryptic file name.

Possible solutions:

1. Use a config-file: Check if a configuration file exists and if so, use the contents for HOST and KEY (JSON, XML, Plain Text)
2. Add parameters: e. g. -h and -k to be able to use a start.bat (Windows) or shell script (Linux) to call the executable with these parameters.

Best regards

title: MacOS <-> Windows10: Keystrokes in client when RustDesk window out of focus
body:
<!-- Hey there, thank you for creating an issue! -->

**Describe the bug you encountered:**
Situation 1:
I am accessing a Windows 10 client (desktop app) (WC1) from a MacOS machine (desktop app) (MC2). 
When I tab out of an active RustDesk window to let's say Firefox on MC2 (CMD+Tab) two unintended things happen:
1. In the remote session on WC1 'CMD+Tab' triggers 'WIN+Tab': This results in opening the 'Task View' [0] which is weird but not tragic.
2. When the remote user in front of WC1 clicks on one of the tasks from the 'Task View' or in other words focuses on one window on WC1 (i.e. Notepad), all keystrokes of MC2 get transfered to WC1. Which could be catastrophic and result in data loss. Ways to stop this are either to disconnect RustDesk or refocus to RustDesk on MC2 and use the Mac Taskbar to switch to another app.

To reproduce it is not enough to CMD+Tab to RustDesk and away. You have to be active in the RustDesk window. 

Situation 2:
Testing the other way around (WC1 is accessing MC2) we have a different outcome:
Focused on the RustDesk window on WC1 the keyboard shortcut WIN+TAB does not open any app switcher on both systems. It does however switch between the last two active windows on MC2. WIN+ALT does nothing. 
...

**What did you expect to happen instead?**
Situation 1:
For 1.: I would expect that either CMD+Tab changes focus on MC2 and nothing happens on WC1 OR CMD+Tab opens the 'Task View' on WC1 and focus does not change on MC2.

For 2: As soon as there is no focus on the RustDesk app, no keystrokes or mouse movements or anything should get mirrored to the remote session. 

Situation 2:
I would expect that either WIN+Tab opens the 'Task View' on WC1 and nothing happens on MC2 OR WIN+Tab opens the 'App Switcher' on MC2 and nothing happens on WC1. Same for ALT+TAB.
...


**How did you install `RustDesk`?**

<!-- GitHub release, build from source, Windows portable version, etc. -->
On MC2: brew install rustdesk
On WC1: choco install rustdesk

---

**RustDesk version and environment**
MC2: RustDesk v1.1.9
WC1: RustDesk v1.1.9
<!--
In order to reproduce your issue, please add some information about the environment
in which you're running RustDesk.
-->
MC2: MacOS 10.15.7
WC1: Win10 21H2

title: [Sugestions] for Rustdesk Android
body:
So I have been using RustDesk for a while, feeling really satisfied that I can use my own VPS to remote control. But something is disturbing when using Android client (haven't tested iOS, don't know if its common). I will write them here for you developers.

1. If we zoom the image of remote control, it will look like this one: 
[Screenshot](https://nas1.ads-network.top/index.php/s/xin4iEgAamskRQb)
(In this case, its left top corner is aligned with the Android device's screen)

The reason why it is disturbing, is the way virtual mouse moves. When zoomed. the image automatically move with the mouse, this one is okay; But the problem is, the image simply just horizonally do the exact same motion as the mouse do(keeping the mouse in the horizonal center of screen). This makes the experience of zoomed views a Marksman one - Imagine that you are going to click a button. The mouse (and image) moves there and there, just don't fit in the right postion!

As there are no settings related to this one, I suggest to add some changes in a future update. We can set up an forbidden area when the image is zoomed - and when mouse is hiting it, the image moves, staying the mouse at the edge of the area. It is not quite hard to modify, so I am looking forward to this one :-)

2. Allow users to customize gestures
Some gestures with multiple fingers really don't fit my habits. Such as the mouse wheel with 3-fingers. I can never bare putting 3 fingers at a same time a phone for such a simple task. The suggestion is, allow us to customize it. For myself, I will delete to two finger moving canvas one and change it into mouse wheel task. 

3. [SELF-HOST COMMON] 
For some reasons, I realized that Rustdesk binds multiple ports at once. This at first, caused a lot of problems when I think that rbbr/rbbs binded the port I specified. But in fact they do bind more, , and there were no loggings telling me that. It resolved as I modified the ports the bind, but it is still disturbing. 

That is all for now. Thanks you guys for your efforts making this project!

title: Disable Background Service / Autostart (Windows/Linux)
body:
Hi,
i found rustdesk and it works great.

I have one problem, this is the running background service which cant be killed or will be automatic started on system startup.
On Linux i disabled the server connection and closed rustdesk, still the service persists. Also starts on the startup again.
I really don't like this, that i never definitly know if something is running in the background.
I want to control what starts on my client and i want to enable such a thing first.

I couldnt find a way to disable the service on startup in the app and also dont find a way to close the app completely the app. (Linux)
On Windows i dont know if the app closes correctly every time. In my tests some times the app closes completly and sometimes it runs in the background.

Feature Request:
Create a Quit Application Button (To Close all background Services)
Create a Host Startup Toggle in the Rustdesk App. (Windows/Linux)

title: Dear author, would you consider checking the parameters that can directly transfer files by default?
body: Because if you don’t pay attention when transferring files remotely, you need to copy the file twice each time, which is a bit cumbersome, or put this parameter in the configuration file and let the user choose whether it needs to be checked by default.

title: 【macos】 change the menu shortcut key please.  It conflicts with system default shortcut
body: The default action of “command+a ” is “select all”. plz change menu shortcut key to some else. or just remove it. and, change the shortcut of "Settings" to "command+," . that's the habit of macos

title: Modify screen resolution
body:
The remote target I connect to is the `win10 server`. Usually, the system is inactive and does not support modifying the resolution. Moreover, the server system does not contain a graphics card program, and the default resolution is `800*600`.
Can you support changing the screen resolution like `sunlogin` or `todesk `or `mstsc`.

title: Improve application's accessibility for screenreaders
body:
I would like RustDesk to get much better screenreader accessibility support. I have tested the program and below are my findings
## Windows client
The application is barely usable with a screenreader. I have tested the program with [Zhengdu Screenreader](http://zdsr.com) and the results were much better than with more broadly used [NVDA](https://nvaccess.org). Most problems however are not related to the screenreader in question but to how the interface is being rendered. Iunderstand that Sciter is used as an Ui library in RustDesk,however from what I have read on their site, Microsoft User Interface Automation (UIA) is supported, however the interface needs to be updated appropriately, namely Text Boxes should get a proper label, using the `<label>` HTML tags. The same thing happens inside the file transfering dialog. The lists and text boxeshave no appropriatelabel, but what's more the context menu is not read at all. Moreover there seem to be a few options which are completely unreachable with the keyboard, namely theaddress book and recently connected devices list. They seem to be completely excluded from the TAB navigation order.

## MacOS client

The MacOS client is completely inaccessible, however that seemst o be related to Sciter directly. I post this info here for the sae of completeness...

## mobile clients

Mobile clients are made with Flutter which comes with great support for accessibility by default, howeve rsome stuff have tobe made available by the developer. The problem with Rustdesk mobile clients are buttons which haveonly images as their labels, so screenreaders cannot read the,. Of course, you do not need to add texts to your buttons for all users, Flutter comes with a great "SemanticLabel" widget which can be used for giving graphical controls meaningful names. More about this widget can be read [here](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/Icon/semanticLabel.html)
The same technique can be added in order to support accessibility in the web client.

title: Define random generated password max length through installer flag
body: Hi team, loving the work you're all doing here. I think it would be awesome to see an installer flag similar to the "--silent-install" one that would define the max lengths of passwords rustdesk generates. For example, Something like  "--password-length=10" that would cause a fresh install to generate 10 character long passwords. I'm a bit concerned 6 characters consisting of just letters may not be enough entropy to prevent brute force attacks, Especially now with the new local network discovery feature in mid to large scale deployments. Granted, I dont thing this is something that's a very likely vector of attack for Rustdesk currently, but if the software becomes more popular over time I can see it becoming an issue.

title: Right ctrl button not working in combination with another key
body:
**Describe the bug you encountered:**
The right ctrl button is not working as expected when used in combination with another key. (tested using a regular US English keyboard)

For example, on Chrome browser, in regular use on the client computer (not through Rustdesk), pressing either left ctrl + P, or right ctrl + P, both pops up the window to Print the page.

On the remote computer in Rustdesk, only left ctrl + P works. Right ctrl + P is not working - instead, it only types the letter "p" (as if only P was pressed without ctrl).

The right ctrl button by itself is being detected correctly, e.g. in Windows Explorer, if I hold right ctrl and click and drag a file to make a copy, it works fine, just the same way it would with left ctrl. It is only when right ctrl is used in combination with another key (e.g. Right Ctrl+P), the combination doesn't work.

For some situations/key combinations (e.g. moving around and selecting in an Excel spreadsheet, using arrows, shift+arrows, ctrl+arrows) it's easier to use the right ctrl button to do this (with the left hand) and not need to take my right hand off the mouse - so while it is less common to use the right ctrl button, in some situations it's useful.
